generation.method: checked runtime inventory plus exact npm tarballs extracted into a temporary node_modules tree
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per act scenario and mode

probeModes.reason: Default Node development exposes React.act and records the deprecation wrapper's normal delegation behavior.
Default Node production records the published test-utils wrapper when the React production entrypoint omits React.act.
The react-server condition leaves react-dom/test-utils public, while the React server entrypoint omits React.act.
Production react-server records the same public wrapper against the production React server entrypoint.

scenarios.area: test-utils act export and public React.act relationship
captures: dynamic import namespace keys and descriptors
act function name, length, and own-key shape
React.act presence across default, production, and react-server modes
react-dom/test-utils act identity relative to React.act

scenarios.area: CommonJS and dynamic import descriptor behavior
captures: dynamic import namespace assignment and delete failures
default and module.exports interop after CommonJS mutation
callback invocation count when React.act is available
callback non-invocation when React.act is absent
observable parity with React.act in default development
production and react-server absence of React.act
observable parity with React.act in default development
React.act absence errors before callback invocation
observable parity with React.act in default development
function with then property treated as sync return
object with non-function then treated as sync return

scenarios.area: async act without awaiting returned thenable

intentionalGaps.reason: This oracle records pinned React DOM test-utils behavior only; local Fast React React DOM behavior is not compared in this worker slice.
The scenarios avoid DOM roots, component rendering, fake timers, Suspense rendering, and renderer work queues; those need separate renderer-backed oracles.
The oracle observes the relationship to public React.act where exported, but implementing React.act itself belongs to the React package/runtime behavior surface.
